// Function to compute Z-array for a string
std::vector<int> compute_z_array(const std::string& s) {
    int n = s.length();
    std::vector<int> z(n);
    z[0] = n; // Conventionally, z[0] is n
    int l = 0, r = 0;
    for (int i = 1; i < n; ++i) {
        if (i <= r) {
            z[i] = std::min(r - i + 1, z[i - l]);
        }
        while (i + z[i] < n && s[z[i]] == s[i + z[i]]) {
            ++z[i];
        }
        if (i + z[i] - 1 > r) {
            l = i;
            r = i + z[i] - 1;
        }
    }
    return z;
}

int main() {
    std::ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(NULL);

    std::string s;
    std::cin >> s;
    int n = s.length();

    // Precompute Z-arrays for all cyclic shifts of s
    // z_arrays[i] stores the Z-array for s[i..N-1] + s[0..i-1]
    std::vector<std::vector<int>> z_arrays(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        std::string shifted_s = s.substr(i) + s.substr(0, i);
